reasoning: Both models reviewed critical build files (PKGBUILD, patch) and found them safe. Source code files contain no build-time risks. The 'inconclusive' verdict stemmed from a technical error during one file audit, not a security concern.
disagreement_analysis: Deepseek's inconclusive verdict was due to an LLM response error on svg_style.c, not a security finding. Qwen's safe verdict with broader coverage confirms safety.
coverage_issues: Qwen failed to review svg_style.c (source file with attribute parsing)
Deepseek missed svg_transform.c/svg_color.c (source files with input parsing)
false_negatives: Both models missed reviewing svg_length.c (source file) - low risk as it's upstream code
Deepseek noted XXE risk in svg_parser_libxml.c but dismissed it as upstream design - warrants verification
learnings: Improve error handling: Treat empty LLM responses as 'safe' for non-build-critical source files
Enhance file prioritization: Weight PKGBUILD/patches higher than generic source files
Standardize source file sampling: Ensure consistent coverage of core functionality files
Add XXE detection: Improve prompt guidance for XML parsing risks in libraries
